findings.title: Notification Priority Levels Implementation
findings.description: Implementing a three-tier priority system for notifications requires changes to the notification rendering logic and user interface components. This will also necessitate updates to the backend to support priority levels.
assumptions: The current notification system can be modified to include priority levels.

evidence.excerpt: Propose a three-tier priority system that visually differentiates urgency.

recommendations: Design and implement backend changes to support priority levels.

findings.title: Push Notification Delivery Issues on Android 14
findings.description: The failure of push notifications on Android 14 devices poses a significant risk to user engagement and satisfaction. This issue needs to be addressed urgently to prevent further user attrition.
assumptions: The issue is limited to Android 14 and can be resolved with a software update.

evidence.excerpt: Several users on Android 14 report that push notifications silently fail.

recommendations: Investigate and resolve the push notification delivery issue for Android 14.

findings.title: Implement ML-based Notification Batching
findings.description: The implementation of ML-based notification batching will require a robust machine learning model and integration with user engagement data. This is a complex task that should be planned for a later phase after addressing more critical issues.
assumptions: The ML model can be trained effectively with existing user data.

evidence.excerpt: Batch low-priority notifications into a digest delivered at optimal times.

recommendations: Plan for the development of the ML model and integration in V2.

findings.title: User Preference Page for Notification Categories
findings.description: Creating a user preference page requires a new API endpoint and a React component. This will also depend on the existing user settings infrastructure.
assumptions: The existing user settings infrastructure can support new preferences.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

recommendations: Develop the API endpoint and React component for user preferences.

findings.title: Notification Delivery Latency Reduction
findings.description: Reducing notification delivery latency from p95 340ms to under 200ms is critical for performance. This requires architectural changes to the delivery pipeline, which may introduce risks if not properly managed.
assumptions: The proposed architectural changes will lead to the desired latency reduction.

evidence.excerpt: Root cause appears to be synchronous database writes in the delivery pipeline.

recommendations: Implement async writes and batch inserts as proposed.

summary: The analysis identifies critical dependencies and risks associated with the SmartNotify project. Key components such as notification priority levels and delivery latency reduction are prioritized for the MVP and V1 phases, while ML-based batching is recommended for V2. Immediate attention is required for push notification issues on Android 14, which is a blocking risk.
